content.problemStatement:
JJ has an integer KKK. JJ also has an array AAA of length NNN where (0‚â§Ai<2K)(0 \le A_i \lt 2^K)(0‚â§Ai‚Äã<2K). He can perform the following operations on AAA any number of times (possibly zero):


Select iii and xxx such that 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N and 0‚â§x<2K0 \le x \lt 2^K0‚â§x<2K and for each 1‚â§j‚â§i1 \le j \le i1‚â§j‚â§i, set Aj:=Aj&xA_j := A_j \And xAj‚Äã:=Aj‚Äã&x.
Select iii and xxx such that 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N and 0‚â§x<2K0 \le x \lt 2^K0‚â§x<2K and for each i‚â§j‚â§Ni \le j \le Ni‚â§j‚â§N, set Aj:=Aj‚à£xA_j := A_j \mid xAj‚Äã:=Aj‚Äã‚à£x.
Here, &\And& denotes the bitwise AND operation and ‚à£\mid‚à£ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

Determine the number of different arrays JJ can obtain by applying the above operations zero or more times. Since the answer can be large, output the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Two arrays PPP and QQQ are said to be different if there exists any index iii such that Pi‚âÝQiP_i \neq Q_iPi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Qi‚Äã.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the size of the array AAA and the parameter KKK mentioned in the statement.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of different arrays JJ can obtain, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: [0,0][0, 0][0,0], [0,1][0, 1][0,1], [1,0][1, 0][1,0] and [1,1][1, 1][1,1] are the arrays which can be obtained from A=[1,0]A = [1, 0]A=[1,0].
Test case 222: [0,0][0, 0][0,0], [0,1][0, 1][0,1], and [1,1][1, 1][1,1] are the arrays which can be obtained from A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1]. Note that we cannot obtain [1,0][1, 0][1,0].
